title: root-less containers look for root-full paths?
body:
Hi gays.

I've recently updated rpm packages on my c10 and there were, amongst many more, these:

```crun-1.28-1.el10.x86_64
containers-common-6.0-2.el10.noarch
containers-common-extra-6.0-2.el10.noarch
systemd-container-257-28.el10.x86_64
podman-6.0.0-1.el10.x86_64
```

I mention it for I cannot think of any other reason why on all my systems, (suddenly) rootless users (via IPA backend) now fail, when I do anything with podman toolbox I get:

```
WARN[0000] RunRoot is pointing to a path (/run/containers/storage) which is not writable. Most likely podman will fail.: permission denied 
Error: cannot evaluate symlinks on DB run root path "/run/containers/storage": lstat /run/containers/storage: permission denied
```

I put it here, as a **bug** for I really think it's new versions of core &| dependent component - anybody?
many thanks, L.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

comments.body:
Is this change to functionality a default behaviour now?
I presume that installation package should take care of the path(s) ? that path did not exist on my Centos.
This "fix" seems to be the resolution of the issue.
Yes that is a breaking change in Podman 6
